'''R2-T0''' was an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] owned by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] [[Watto/Legends|Watto]], who made it available for sale from [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Wald's Parts|junk shop]] in the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The Toydarian also rented the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]] around the port, and in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], R2-T0 worked with the [[Welding droid/Legends|welding droid]] [[WEL-1708]] in the streets of Mos Espa.

==Biography==
R2-T0<ref name="CSWE" /> was an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]]<ref name="Snapshot" /> owned by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Watto/Legends|Watto]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] who ran a [[Wald's Parts|shop]] in [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="TPMEVD">''[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] was among those available for sale in the shop,<ref name="Hasbro">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=Droid Factory Pack|pack=#5: Watto & R2-T0|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tlcDF5.asp}}</ref> and was also rented out to [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]] around Mos Espa by Watto.<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|I}} to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> R2-T0 worked with the<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> [[Welding droid/Legends|welding droid]] [[WEL-1708]] on a piece of machinery in the streets of Mos Espa. Both droids were using their [[Limb/Legends|manipulators]] to hold up a metal rod against the machinery<ref name="TPMEVD" /> when [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], and the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] passed by them.<ref name="TPM" />

==Characteristics==
R2-T0 stood 0.96 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] (3 [[Foot/Legends|feet]], 2 inches) tall<ref name="Characterisitcs" /> and had [[Color/Legends|gray]] and green plating and a black [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]]. The droid was equipped with a small [[Lamp/Legends|lamp]] attached to the top of its dome.<ref name="CSWE" /> Like all [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]], it was manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]].<ref name="Characterisitcs" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Wattosastro.jpg|thumb|right|200px|R2-T0 appeared in Episode I.]]
R2-T0 appeared in the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The droid was not identified in the film,<ref name="TPM" /> but was given a name when [[Hasbro]] released a figure of it packaged with Watto as part of the [[Star Wars: Droid Factory (toy line)|''Star Wars: Droid Factory'']] line in the ''[[Star Wars: The Legacy Collection]]'' series of figures in [[2008]].<ref name="Hasbro" />
